steps.prompt: Which words are verbs in this sentence?

hints.'Omne' and 'punctum': 'Omne' is an adjective and 'punctum' is a noun — look for words with verb endings
hints.'punctum' and 'qui': 'punctum' is a noun meaning 'point' and 'qui' is a relative pronoun
hints.'Omne' and 'miscuit': 'Omne' is an adjective meaning 'every' — only 'miscuit' is correct from this pair

hints.Present: Present of 'ferre' would be 'fert' — 'tulit' uses the perfect stem 'tul-'
hints.Imperfect: Imperfect of 'ferre' would be 'ferebat' — 'tulit' uses the perfect stem 'tul-'
hints.Pluperfect: Pluperfect of 'ferre' would be 'tulerat' — 'tulit' lacks the -era- pluperfect marker

hints.sum, esse, fui, futurum: These are the principal parts of 'esse' (to be)
hints.do, dare, dedi, datum: These are the principal parts of 'dare' (to give)
hints.ago, agere, egi, actum: These are the principal parts of 'agere' (to do/drive)

hints.Nominative: While 'omne' can be nominative neuter, here it modifies 'punctum' as direct object of 'tulit'
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'omnis' neuter would be 'omni' — 'omne' is nominative or accusative
hints.Vocative: 'Omne' is not being used as a form of address here

hints.Nominative: While 'punctum' could be nominative, here it is the direct object of 'tulit'
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'punctum' would be 'puncti' — 'punctum' is nominative or accusative
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'punctum' would be 'puncto' — 'punctum' retains the -um ending

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'puncti' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'puncti' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'puncti' ends in -i

hints.Interrogative adverb: 'qui' as an adverb means 'how' — here it introduces a relative clause
hints.Conjunction: 'qui' is not a conjunction — conjunctions like 'et' or 'sed' join clauses differently

hints.Present: Present of 'miscēre' would be 'miscet' — 'miscuit' has the perfect -ui- ending
hints.Imperfect: Imperfect of 'miscēre' would be 'miscebat' — 'miscuit' has the perfect -ui- ending
hints.Future: Future of 'miscēre' would be 'miscebit' — 'miscuit' has the perfect -ui- ending

hints.video, vidēre, vidi, visum: These are the principal parts of 'vidēre' (to see)
hints.habeo, habēre, habui, habitum: These are the principal parts of 'habēre' (to have)
hints.moneo, monēre, monui, monitum: These are the principal parts of 'monēre' (to warn)

translation: He wins every point who mixes
